special_mechanics.name: Wall Climb (SNES only)
special_mechanics.description: Wolverine can climb on any wall or ceiling with his claws

special_mechanics.name: Springing Leap (SNES only)
special_mechanics.description: Wolverine can make springing leaps from walls/ceilings

special_mechanics.name: Double Jump (Genesis only)
special_mechanics.description: Wolverine performs a double jump that rolls into a ball and travels a short distance

special_mechanics.name: Lunge Attack (Genesis only)
special_mechanics.description: Attack that can be used to jump great distances as well as attack

special_mechanics.name: Quick Roll (Genesis only)
special_mechanics.description: Rolls great distance along ground and allows Wolverine to go under enemy bullets

special_mechanics.name: Healing Factor
special_mechanics.description: Health recovers over time after taking damage. SNES version is slower process; Genesis version includes countdown timer to show distance from Elsie-Dee

tier_notes: Tier 3 data: All information is qualitative/descriptive from gameplay documentation. No precise numerical physics parameters (velocities, acceleration values, frame data, or sub-pixel precision) are documented in available sources. SNES and Genesis versions have significantly different move sets and mechanics despite similar overall structure.
